title: plantago subulata subsp. atlantis
intro: Plantago subulata subsp. atlantis, also known as the Atlantic plantain, is a subspecies of the plantain family, characterized by its small, oval-shaped leaves and delicate stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects, which help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce swelling, and fight infections. The health benefits of Plantago subulata subsp. atlantis include treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as alleviating respiratory problems, like bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, which are often prepared by infusing the leaves in hot water or mixing them with other herbs to create a medicinal remedy.
